story.text: As a developer with over five years of experience and a passion for entrepreneurship, I enjoy transforming ideas into concrete products by combining strategy and technique. My goal is to support startups and scale-ups with sharp expertise and a pragmatic approach to evolve their products.

hero.name: HUGO BAYOUD
hero.title: Full Stack Web & Mobile Developer
hero.description: Specialized in the JavaScript ecosystem. Strong UX/UI sensitivity to create performant and intuitive applications.

projects.title: Professional experience
projects.subTitle: Companies and missions where I contributed as a developer or co-founder—from product work to technical leadership.

invstore.myRole: Co-founder & Full-Stack Web and Mobile Developer
invstore.companyDescription: Invstore connects private individuals with financial professionals.
invstore.myRoleShortDescription: Development of a mobile app for private individuals, as well as a secure web platform enabling financial professionals to access their prospective clients.
invstore.myRoleLongDescription:
Following the commercial failure of Sowhat, my business partner and I decided to make a strategic pivot in order to develop a new solution focused on the journey of an individual looking for a wealth management adviser.

We have raised €110,000 from a family office to carry out a proof of concept for this new idea.

Thanks to the experience I’ve gained, I developed the solution in less than three months, along with the web platform that enables professionals – bankers, wealth managers and other FinTech firms – to contact their prospective clients.

However, in the weeks that followed, we were unable to meet our user targets. We therefore discontinued the service.

Key technologies: UI/UX design, full-stack development, REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, Node.js, JavaScript, TypeScript, NestJS, mobile development, React Native with Expo, CI/CD, deployment to the App Store and Google Play, website development, Next.js, Supabase, Microsoft Azure, GitHub Actions.

sapio.companyDescription: Thetys Lab is the studio behind Sapio, a mobile app that makes general knowledge as addictive as a game, often described as the ‘Duolingo of general knowledge’.
sapio.myRoleShortDescription: A six-month project to help Sapio grow from 0 to 50,000 users: database architecture, backend, an advanced push notification system and gamification features.
sapio.myRoleLongDescription:
I was responsible for the technical foundations of the app: the data model, growth-oriented architecture, development of a large part of the backend, React Native application logic, gamified interfaces, new game modes and an advanced push notification system.

The work was organised and documented in close collaboration with the team to ensure a smooth launch within a tight deadline.

Key technologies: Full-stack development, REST APIs, Node.js, JavaScript, TypeScript, mobile development, React Native with Expo, CI/CD, GitHub, Firebase, Cloud Functions, Expo Push Notifications.

sowhat.companyDescription: Sowhat is a FinTech company that uses Open Finance to help users manage their personal finances and assets more effectively.
sowhat.myRoleShortDescription: Co-founder of Sowhat, a FinTech company harnessing the opportunities offered by Open Finance and PSD2 to develop a personal finance app: budget management, banking monitoring and wealth management.
sowhat.myRoleLongDescription:
Before starting to develop the Sowhat app, my business partner and I worked on designing the mock-ups using Figma. We spent several months defining a user interface that met our needs and an approach tailored to the requirements we had identified.

I then developed the app’s first MVP, handling every stage of the process - from the initial idea through to deployment on the app stores - in nine months.

After several months of operation and bug fixes, we realised that we hadn’t yet found our niche. The sowhat-app.com website is no longer online and has been replaced by invstore.fr, a second project developed with my business partner.

lepto.companyDescription: Lepto is a mobile app designed for managing group expenses. Unlike existing solutions such as Tricount, Lepto stands out thanks to its modern, intuitive and user-friendly interface, designed with holidays and nights out with friends in mind. The app makes it easy to calculate reimbursements automatically and helps to minimise the stress associated with financial discussions within a group.
lepto.myRoleShortDescription: Design and development of the backend for Lepto, a mobile app designed for managing group expenses.
lepto.myRoleLongDescription:
As CTO, I bridged the gap between the product vision and technical implementation: choosing the tech stack, defining the architecture, delivering end-to-end features, and maintaining technical quality as usage grew.

The main challenge was to offer a more seamless and intuitive experience than standard reimbursement apps, thanks to a well-designed UI/UX and a robust technical foundation.

Key technologies: UI/UX design, backend development, REST APIs, Node.js, JavaScript, NestJS, CI/CD, deployment to the App Store and Google Play, social media, video editing.

startupoklm.companyDescription: Startup OKLM is a media outlet dedicated to entrepreneurship. Together with my business partner, we have interviewed well-known entrepreneurs such as Ilan Benhaim, founder of Veepee, and Anthony Bourbon, founder of Feed and Blast.club. Our content has reached a combined audience of over 30,000 followers across LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok and Twitch.
startupoklm.myRoleShortDescription: Responsible for the production and management of video content: filming, editing and publishing on various platforms (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Twitch).
startupoklm.myRoleLongDescription:
My role focused on the production and management of video content. I was responsible for filming, editing and publishing videos across various platforms.

This experience has enabled me to develop skills in social media communication strategy, whilst gaining a thorough understanding of the specific features of each platform in order to maximise engagement and visibility.

Key skills: Video production, video editing, managing social media posts, adapting content to suit the requirements of each platform, and preparing interviews with public figures.

mybus.companyDescription: Monkey Factory is developing MyBus, an app that brings together various modes of transport – bikes, scooters, buses, carpooling, etc. – to simplify urban travel.
mybus.myRoleShortDescription: Backend developer working on a monorepo: integrating a route-planning system and contributing to the digitisation of travel tickets.
mybus.myRoleLongDescription:
I integrated a high-performance route-planning subsystem and helped to improve the reliability of the backend to enable rapid iterations, against a backdrop of growing client numbers, particularly from cities and urban areas.

The backend was organised as a monorepo, featuring a GraphQL API and a REST API. I worked as part of a small team, within an agile framework structured around two-week sprints.

Key technologies: JavaScript, Node.js, NestJS, GraphQL, REST API, XML, GitLab, CI/CD, DevOps, agile methodology.

skills.subTitle: The technologies and applications I use daily.

podcast.title: Podcast
podcast.description: Invited to Fabien Serra's microphone on Tizy Media, I discuss my journey in development, FinTechs, and entrepreneurship.

jbThouard.title: Jean Baptiste Thouard
jbThouard.subtitle: Co-founder & CTO Thetys Lab
jbThouard.description:
We had the opportunity to work with Hugo for six months (from April to September 2025), taking Sapio – our general knowledge mobile app – from initial design through to launch.

He demonstrated excellent command of React Native and its wider ecosystem, both on the backend and frontend: building the entire application logic, setting up a large part of the backend, implementing an advanced push notification system, and designing gamified interfaces along with new game modes.

We particularly valued Hugo’s organisational skills (whether working independently or within the team), his responsiveness, and the positive attitude he consistently brought to the project.

His work is always clear, well-structured, and properly documented.

Thanks to his contribution, we were able to release a stable version of our app in record time.
